Their work became far more urgent in the late 1990s, when the pyrimethamine combination suddenly started to fail in Kenya and other countries, as the parasite mutated and became resistant.
Tests on more than 2, 000 children in several African countries now show that Lapdap cures significantly more malaria cases than pyrimethamine-sulfadoxine and can help in tough cases where it fails.
